Learning Python From a JavaScript Background (2): The Origin Story
Learning Python From a JavaScript Background

Every programming language has its own little birth story. JavaScript's is famously chaotic (the whole "Java vs JavaScript is like dog vs hot dog" thing), so what about Python? Is its history just as fun? This post takes a quick look at where Python came from, why it's named after a comedy troupe, and what makes its syntax so clean.
Learn Python From a JavaScript Dev's Angle (1) - Table of Contents and Rambling
Learn Python From a JavaScript Angle

This is my third Ironman challenge this year and my fourth Ironman series overall. Honestly I'm a little scared I won't finish this time, because this series is pretty much learning Python from scratch — but from a JavaScript developer's point of view. Here's the table of contents and a bit of rambling to kick things off.
Stop Letting Them Quietly Collect My Data — Disabling Personalized Ads on Google and Facebook

Heads up — this isn't a "block all ads" post. It's a "limit how much these platforms quietly collect about you" post. Covers Google's ad personalization controls (the activity controls + ad settings page), my actual recommendation (counterintuitively — keep personalization on but tune it), and Facebook's privacy checkup for hiding specific advertisers and topics.
JavaScript Core (64) - ES6: Arrow Functions - A Quick Intro

Time for a brand new chapter — arrow functions. This is a quick intro to the ES6 arrow function syntax: how it compares to a regular function expression, the shorthand tricks you can use to drop the return and the braces, and the one rule about when you can (and can't) skip the parameter parentheses.
Reset CSS vs Normalize.css — The Difference
Haven't written a CSS post in ages. Here's a quick look at the differences between Reset CSS and Normalize.css.
Let & Const Quiz: Can You Predict the Output Before Looking? (JS Core #63)

Time for some exercises on let and const. As always, the advice is the same: think each question through carefully before you peek at the answer. Ten quick questions covering the temporal dead zone, const with arrays and objects, hoisting, and which ESLint style to pick.
Quickly Clear Old Form Data or Stale Email History from Your Browser

Browsers love to suggest every email and username you (or anyone using your computer) ever typed. This post covers the Shift+Delete trick to remove individual autocomplete entries in Firefox and Chrome without nuking your whole browsing history.
JavaScript Core (62) — ES6 Let & Const: Let ESLint Be Your Study Buddy

If you want to take your JavaScript up a level, I'd genuinely suggest giving ESLint a try. This post covers what ESLint is, why the Airbnb and Standard style guides matter, and a concrete reason why you shouldn't mix var with ES6's let and const.
Using Firefox for Frontend Dev: Features and Advantages

People keep asking what browser I use and why it isn't Chrome. So here's a quick post on Firefox's frontend dev features — better-than-Chrome Flex tooling, CSS Grid inspection, event listener inspection per element, accessibility tools like color blindness simulation, in-browser font tools, and an AJAX inspector that lets you edit and resend requests directly.
Discord Role Bot Tutorial: Auto-Assign Roles via Invite Links (Invite-Role Bot)

When you want new members to automatically get a specific role based on which invite link they used, vanilla Discord can't do it on its own — you need a bot. This post walks through using Invite-Role Bot to wire invite links to roles.
